svg.text: ECO-SIM: Buriti Palm Palm Palm × Ectomycorrhizal Fungi - Flood-Pulse Hyphal Network

g.text: When the vereda floods (Nov–Mar), soil oxygen
explosively, colonising new Buriti root tips
within days, bridging palms up to 40 m apart.
In the dry season, hyphae lyse near the surface.
Fungi form sporocarps eaten by armadillos
that redistribute spores across the Cerrado.
